qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Mirror address update bypass permits local-network SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a mirror settings validation bypass in `internal/route/repo/setting.go` and `internal/database/mirror.go`; the advisory is option injection during release deletion in `internal/database/release.go` when `git tag -d` receives a dash-prefixed tag name.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Upload finalize follows intermediate symlinks and writes outside the repository
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an out-of-repository file write in the upload finalize path in `internal/database/repo_editor.go`; the advisory is option injection during release deletion in `internal/database/release.go` when `git tag -d` receives a dash-prefixed tag name.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-v9vm-r24h-6rqm is specifically the release deletion path in `internal/database/release.go` calling `git tag -d` with `rel.TagName` and no end-of-options separator, while the session's qualifying findings target unrelated mirror-sync SSRF and upload symlink file-write paths.
evidence: The advisory names `internal/database/release.go` and the vulnerable call `process.ExecDir(..., "git", "tag", "-d", rel.TagName)`, with the incomplete prior mitigation `strings.TrimLeft(r.TagName, "-")` only on a creation path.

The `mirror_address_local_network_ssrf` finding targets `internal/route/repo/setting.go` writing `mirror_address` through `Mirror.SaveAddress` and `internal/database/mirror.go` later reaching `git.IsURLAccessible` and `git remote update`; it does not involve release deletion, tag names, or Git option parsing.

The `upload_symlink_file_write` finding targets upload handling in `internal/route/repo/editor.go`, `internal/pathutil/pathutil.go`, and `internal/database/repo_editor.go`, where an intermediate symlink is followed during file creation; it does not involve release deletion, tag names, or Git option parsing.
